jokes.text: This house feels like a hotel to me.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl are moving into a new house
jokes.punchline: The house they own feels impersonal like a hotel
jokes.explanation: Sets tone of domestic discontent, efficient character moment

jokes.text: That was a guy? I thought it was a girl.
jokes.setup: Learning Cheryl is talking to Ted Danson
jokes.punchline: Larry confused Ted's voice for a woman's
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character specificity in Larry's petty observation

jokes.text: That's like sex for platonic friends.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ted and Cheryl's flirtatious tapping
jokes.punchline: Equating innocent touching to sexual substitute
jokes.explanation: Clever observation with quotable phrasing and unexpected analogy

jokes.text: You know, they're naming a wing after me.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about NRDC event
jokes.punchline: Bragging about having a wing named after him
jokes.explanation: Sets up theme of public recognition but fairly standard rich guy boasting

jokes.text: No, he lives here in L.A.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Leon was in the hurricane
jokes.punchline: He wasn't displaced, he just showed up anyway
jokes.explanation: Reveals Leon is mooching, not a hurricane victim, nice character beat

jokes.text: Was he in the hurricane? - No, he lives here in L.A.
jokes.setup: Larry assuming Leon is a hurricane refugee needing shelter
jokes.punchline: Leon actually just lives locally and came to visit
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection revealing Larry's assumptions about the situation

jokes.text: Loretta put her foot in it, turned it around, and kicked you in your own asshole.
jokes.setup: Praising Loretta's cooking
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor about food being so good it assaults you
jokes.explanation: Leon's distinctive voice with colorful, unexpected metaphor construction

jokes.text: I'm gonna think about eating it.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests getting rid of unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Dry response showing she'll ignore health advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with economy, character-specific response

jokes.text: Like when a guy... does his business, and he's alone.
jokes.setup: Cheryl found a stain on Leon's blanket
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemistic description of masturbation
jokes.explanation: Sets up main plot thread with appropriate awkwardness

jokes.text: Hey, everybody in the family have them big ones.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the stain
jokes.punchline: Leon overshares about his family's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate oversharing perfectly fits Leon's character

jokes.text: I don't want some guy staying here masturbating all over the place.
jokes.setup: Larry deciding to confront Leon
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of the situation
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to catastrophize, good character comedy

jokes.text: Ajackalit.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'ejaculate'
jokes.punchline: Leon mispronounces it as 'ajackalit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment, incredibly quotable mispronunciation

jokes.text: What kind of cum was it, first of all?
jokes.setup: Discussion about the cum stain
jokes.punchline: Leon asking for specifics as if there are varieties
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity request shows Leon's unique logic

jokes.text: It couldn't have been mine. Know why? 'cause I gets mine, Larry. I brings the ruckus to the ladies.
jokes.setup: Leon denying the stain is his
jokes.punchline: Boastful explanation about his sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Leon's distinctive voice and swagger, quotable phrasing

jokes.text: What am I doing, jacking off to 'Andy Griffith'? Jacking off to Clara, huh?
jokes.setup: Leon explaining lack of visual stimulation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting masturbating to wholesome TV show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of sexual act with innocent TV show

jokes.text: Nobody wanna jack off to no damn Aunt Bea.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of Andy Griffith Show
jokes.punchline: Definitively ruling out Aunt Bea as sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Leon's distinctive voice and logic

jokes.text: Larry examining the blanket stain closely
jokes.setup: Larry at dry cleaner with stained blanket
jokes.punchline: Larry inappropriately inspecting the stain in public
jokes.explanation: Good visual cringe moment showing Larry's obliviousness

jokes.text: I could give a shit if it was Santa Claus. I'm just trying to get it off.
jokes.setup: Larry insisting it wasn't his stain
jokes.punchline: Worker's professional indifference to whose semen it is
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan response with absurd specificity about Santa

jokes.text: You look very cute when you're all blushy. - You think I'm gonna tell? I'm your friend. - I didn't do it.
jokes.setup: Dry cleaner misreading Larry's embarrassment as endearing
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued insistence despite the intimate tone
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment where dry cleaner becomes overly familiar

jokes.text: That's Joe Pepitone's jersey. I've had that jersey since I was a kid. Number 25.
jokes.setup: Discovering his Yankees jersey is missing
jokes.punchline: Specific attachment to obscure player's jersey
jokes.explanation: Sets up B-plot, shows Larry's neurotic attachment to specific items

jokes.text: That's just the unwritten law of dry cleaning. Everyone knows that.
jokes.setup: Larry upset about lost jersey
jokes.punchline: Worker presenting clothing mix-ups as natural law
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about mundane industry practices

jokes.text: I'll have your semen-covered blanket ready on Wednesday.
jokes.setup: End of dry cleaner interaction
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of the gross blanket
jokes.explanation: Perfect uncomfortable ending to scene with professional detachment

jokes.text: Hey hey, I did it. - Come on, no big deal. - You did it? - Yeah, did it, yep, me.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing the mysterious stain
jokes.punchline: Jeff's casual admission of guilt
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation with Jeff's nonchalant confession

jokes.text: It was Passover at your house. I was drinking a little bit, had some manischewitz, you know? Feeling good, and I was away from the table. I got an erection. I took care of it before I came back.
jokes.setup: Jeff confessing to the masturbation
jokes.punchline: Detailed explanation involving religious holiday
jokes.explanation: Shocking revelation with religious context makes it extra inappropriate

jokes.text: I didn't want to come back to the table with an erection at Passover.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his Passover masturbation
jokes.punchline: Treating erection at dinner table as social faux pas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic treating masturbation as etiquette solution

jokes.text: It's not like Yom Kippur. I would never do it on the High Holidays.
jokes.setup: Jeff defending his Passover masturbation
jokes.punchline: Having religious hierarchy for when masturbation is acceptable
jokes.explanation: Exceptional absurd logic creating religious masturbation hierarchy - becomes cultural reference

jokes.text: They were doing the four questions and I love the four questions.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he rushed back to table
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing Passover ritual over masturbation cleanup
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Jeff's skewed priorities

jokes.text: Come to my house and do it. What do I give a shit? Come on over and whack off. Have a good time.
jokes.setup: Jeff casually suggesting it's no big deal
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically inviting him to masturbate more
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic escalation showing Larry's exasperation

jokes.text: Why are you and Ted always kind of hitting each other all the time? All this playful hitting?
jokes.setup: Larry observing Cheryl and Ted's interaction
jokes.punchline: Larry calling out their flirtatious behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier conversation, shows Larry's obsessiveness

jokes.text: Now it looks like just looks like I did mine for the credit As opposed to, you know, Mr. Wonderful 'Anonymous.'
jokes.setup: Seeing the anonymous wing donation
jokes.punchline: Larry feeling upstaged by anonymous donor
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Larry's competitive nature about charity

jokes.text: You can't have it halfway. You're either anonymous or you're not.
jokes.setup: Learning Ted told some people about his donation
jokes.punchline: Larry's rigid logic about anonymity
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical observation with good economy, very quotable

jokes.text: It's fake philanthropy, and it's faux anonymity.
jokes.setup: Larry's analysis of Ted's donation
jokes.punchline: Alliterative condemnation using 'fake' and 'faux'
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay with alliteration, very economical and quotable

jokes.text: It's fake philanthropy, and it's faux anonymity.
jokes.setup: Larry's continued critique of Ted's donation
jokes.punchline: Alliterative summary of the hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Clever alliterative phrase that crystallizes Larry's complaint

jokes.text: Well, what happens is sometimes they'll give your item to someone else and then that person owns it and walks around in it, and you have no recourse.
jokes.setup: Larry talking to Senator Boxer about dry cleaning
jokes.punchline: Presenting dry cleaning mix-ups as major social issue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of minor annoyance to political issue

jokes.text: Those are somebody else's pants? Well, they're mine now.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about dry cleaning theft
jokes.punchline: Senator admitting she's wearing someone else's pants
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic response showing she's part of the problem

jokes.text: You're part of the problem, senator.
jokes.setup: Senator wearing someone else's pants
jokes.punchline: Larry seriously accusing politician of dry cleaning corruption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character moment showing Larry's persistence

jokes.text: You know, too many people don't do things out of the goodness-- they want the credit.
jokes.setup: Praising Ted's anonymous donation
jokes.punchline: Ironic statement since Larry is standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with Larry present but unacknowledged

jokes.text: You know, you're full of shit. / You're a fucking asshole.
jokes.setup: Argument about anonymity at charity event
jokes.punchline: Public profanity exchange at formal event
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing complete social breakdown

jokes.text: So you know not one person, not one thanked me for the donation.
jokes.setup: Larry upset about lack of recognition
jokes.punchline: Larry's petty focus on not being thanked
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's need for validation

jokes.text: I know who the masturbator was. Jeff.
jokes.setup: Larry revealing the truth to Loretta
jokes.punchline: Dramatic announcement of Jeff as the masturbator
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment with dramatic delivery

jokes.text: I'd rather have the masturbator in the house than the gossiper.
jokes.setup: Learning about Jeff's confession and Ted's gossip
jokes.punchline: Ranking masturbation as less offensive than gossip
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moral hierarchy inversion, extremely quotable and insightful

jokes.text: Hey, Cheryl, guess who jerked off on our blanket? It was Jeff. Isn't that hilarious?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how he would have told Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically presenting gross news as funny
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery showing absurdity of situation

jokes.text: Ted Danson? Yeah, her girlfriend Ted!
jokes.setup: Learning Ted told everyone about the masturbation
jokes.punchline: Referring to Ted as Cheryl's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good callback with gender-bending insult

jokes.text: What a little yenta!
jokes.setup: Jeff upset about Ted's gossiping
jokes.punchline: Calling masculine actor Ted a yenta
jokes.explanation: Good use of Yiddish term for gossipy woman applied to male celebrity

jokes.text: You have wrought your semen outside our home, Jeff!
jokes.setup: Susie learning about Jeff's masturbation
jokes.punchline: Dramatic biblical language for masturbation
jokes.explanation: Exceptional biblical parody language, perfectly fits Susie's dramatic character

jokes.text: You masturbate in this house and this house alone! Maybe a hotel.
jokes.setup: Susie establishing masturbation rules
jokes.punchline: Specific geographic restrictions with hotel exception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant domestic rule-making about masturbation with practical exception

jokes.text: You are banned from my house. Get the fuck out! If he's banned, you're banned!
jokes.setup: Susie learning about the bans
jokes.punchline: Counter-banning Larry in solidarity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing marital solidarity through mutual banning

jokes.text: Where I come from, you see somebody in a sheet, Ghost is not the first thing you think about. You think about K.K.K.
jokes.setup: Larry under a sheet playing with kids
jokes.punchline: Leon's serious reaction about racial implications
jokes.explanation: Powerful dark comedy moment addressing racial trauma through character perspective

jokes.text: That's some fucked-up shit right there, man.
jokes.setup: Learning Jeff was the masturbator
jokes.punchline: Leon's understated reaction to the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction with Leon's distinctive voice

jokes.text: Joe Pepitone up in this motherfucker.
jokes.setup: Leon successfully getting the jersey back
jokes.punchline: Leon's triumphant celebration with street language
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Leon's distinctive celebration style

jokes.text: It was a playful tap!
jokes.setup: Larry hitting Susie
jokes.punchline: Using Ted and Cheryl's flirtation excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that escalates the original joke into physical comedy

jokes.text: Oh my God! There's another Joe Pepitone jersey!
jokes.setup: Finding another jersey after getting the 'wrong' one back
jokes.punchline: Discovering the absurdity of the jersey situation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist on the jersey plot

jokes.text: But that's the law of the dry cleaners. You know, you win some, you lose some.
jokes.setup: Merle defending keeping Larry's jersey
jokes.punchline: Using the dry cleaner law against Larry
jokes.explanation: Good callback with ironic reversal against Larry

jokes.text: I'm on the board at the NRDC and your name's coming off that wall.
jokes.setup: Dispute over the jersey
jokes.punchline: Threatening to remove Larry's name from charity wall
jokes.explanation: Good escalation connecting jersey dispute to main plot

jokes.text: You choked it on Sammy's teddy bear as some kind of revenge masturbatory payback!
jokes.setup: Susie accusing Larry of masturbating on teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Specific accusation of revenge masturbation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with specific 'revenge masturbatory payback' concept

jokes.text: Oh, I know who Anonymous is. It's Ted Danson. He told me, but he told me not to tell anybody.
jokes.setup: Susie asking who did the teddy bear deed
jokes.punchline: Larry using Ted's anonymity pattern for new accusation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that perfectly applies the anonymity theme to new situation

jokes.text: People should know you're anonymous.
jokes.setup: Kathy praising Larry for being anonymous
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statement about publicizing anonymity
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction capturing the absurdity of public anonymity

jokes.text: I'm Anonymous! Larry David is Anonymous!
jokes.setup: Larry being chased, trying to get into building
jokes.punchline: Desperately claiming to be anonymous while shouting his name
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic moment that crystallizes the anonymity absurdity - highly quotable ending
